title: Shri Salasar Balaji Temple
intro: The Salasar Balaji Temple is one of the most significant pilgrimage sites for devotees of Lord Hanuman in India.
description:
Salasar Balaji Temple – Salasar, Rajasthan
Introduction
The Salasar Balaji Temple is one of the most significant pilgrimage sites for devotees of Lord Hanuman in India. Located in the Churu district of Rajasthan, this temple is unique because the idol of Lord Hanuman here features a round face and a mustache and beard, which is quite distinct from other Hanuman temples. It is a major center of faith where millions of devotees flock to seek blessings and offer their prayers.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Salasar Balaji Temple
 Location: Salasar, Churu District, Rajasthan
 Presiding Deity: Lord Hanuman (Balaji form)
 Significance: Famous for the fulfillment of wishes (Mannat).
History and Significance
The temple has a rich history dating back to the 18th century. It is believed that the idol was discovered by a farmer named Mohandas in the village of Asota, who later established the temple in Salasar. The temple holds immense spiritual power for devotees who believe that their distress and obstacles are removed by the grace of Balaji.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air:
 Jaipur International Airport: The nearest major airport (approx. 170–180 km away). Taxis and buses are available from Jaipur to Salasar.
🚆 By Train:
 Sujangarh Railway Station: The nearest railway station (approx. 25–30 km away). From here, you can easily find local taxis or buses to reach the temple.
🛣️ By Road:
 Salasar is well-connected by road to major cities in Rajasthan like Jaipur, Sikar, and Bikaner. Private buses, taxis, and state transport buses are readily available.
Best Time to Visit
 October to March: The weather is pleasant, making it perfect for temple visits and exploring the surrounding area.
 Important Days: Tuesdays and Saturdays are considered very auspicious for worshipping Lord Hanuman.
Temple Timings
🕒 The temple generally remains open from early morning (around 4:00 AM) until late at night (around 10:00 PM).
(Note: Timings can vary during major festivals or special events.)
Nearby Attractions
 Khatu Shyam Ji Temple: Another famous pilgrimage site located within accessible distance.
 Rani Sati Temple (Jhunjhunu): A historic temple famous for its unique architecture and religious significance.
Dress Code
 As with most Hindu temples, modest and traditional attire is recommended.
Facilities for Devotees
 Queue management system.
 Prasad counters.
 Dharamshalas (guesthouses) and hotels nearby for accommodation.
Conclusion
A visit to Salasar Balaji is a deeply spiritual experience. The atmosphere, filled with chants of "Jai Shri Ram" and "Jai Hanuman," provides a sense of peace and strength. For those traveling through Rajasthan, Salasar Balaji stands out as a beacon of devotion and hope.
Since you are building a collection of temple guides, would you like to create an itinerary for a religious tour covering Salasar Balaji and other nearby shrines in Rajasthan?

faq.q1: To which deity is the Salasar Balaji Temple dedicated?
faq.a1: This temple is dedicated to the Balaji form of Lord Shri Hanuman.
faq.q2: Where is the Salasar Balaji Temple located?
faq.a2: It is located in Salasar, in the Churu district of Rajasthan.
faq.q3: What is the Salasar Balaji Temple famous for?
faq.a3: It is famous for the profound devotion to Lord Hanuman, the strong faith that wishes are fulfilled here (mannat), and the large religious fairs held at the temple.
faq.q4: What is the best time to visit Salasar Balaji?
faq.a4: The period from October to March is considered ideal for travel due to the comfortable weather.
faq.q5: What are the major fairs held at Salasar Balaji?
faq.a5: The fairs held during Chaitra Purnima and Ashwin Purnima are the most prominent.
